title: Pod Affinity vs Anti-Affinity
description:
## Pod Affinity vs Anti-Affinity

You want to ensure that **cache** pods are always scheduled on the **same node** as the **app** pods they serve (to reduce latency). Which scheduling rule should you use in the cache pod spec?

options.text: `spec.affinity.nodeAffinity` with a `matchExpressions` for the app pod's node label

explanation: **Pod Affinity** (`spec.affinity.podAffinity`) attracts pods to nodes where matching pods are already running. Use `requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution` for a hard constraint (cache pod MUST co-locate with app pod) vs `preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution` for a soft preference. **Pod Anti-Affinity** does the opposite — spreads pods apart. Tolerations are unrelated to co-location and work with taints.

hints.title: Affinity types
hints.body: podAffinity = attract to same node. podAntiAffinity = repel from same node. nodeAffinity = attract to nodes with specific labels. The `topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname` means 'same node'.
